paper_title: Bee-Pollen Collection and Floral Resource Analysis in Kafrelsheikh Province

methods.description: Preparation of reference slides for pollen grains to facilitate identification of floral origins.

steps.description: Fresh flowers were collected from the plants as buds before anthesis, and reference slides were prepared according to Louveaux et al. (1978).
steps.searchable_summary: collect fresh flower buds and prepare reference slides

steps.description: The anthers were washed out 3 times in a watch glass filled with ether and then were dried.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and dry anthers with ether

steps.description: The pollen grains were transferred to a slide and spread out.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer and spread pollen grains on slide

steps.description: A drop of fructose solution was added to facilitate the transferring, and accelerate the swelling of the pollen grains.
steps.searchable_summary: add fructose solution to pollen on slide

steps.description: The preparation was dried on a warming plate at 40°C and mounted with glycerine gelatine.
steps.searchable_summary: dry and mount pollen preparation with glycerine gelatine

steps.description: The prepared reference slides were stored in a refrigerator.

methods.description: Collection and analysis of bee-pollen to study the fluctuation of collected pollen throughout the year.

steps.description: Five colonies of hybrid Carniolan honeybees were selected in each city to study the fluctuation of collected bee-pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: select honeybee colonies for pollen collection study

steps.description: Pollen traps with efficiency of 25% were fitted on the entrance of the hives in each city.
Pollen loads were collected from pollen traps every two days.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen loads from traps bi-daily

steps.description: Pollen loads were dried at room temperature in a shady place for two hrs to make the separation easy.
steps.searchable_summary: dry pollen loads at room temperature

steps.description: The pollen loads were hand-sorted according to their color and appearance, then referred to their sources, and were weighted using an electrical balance.
steps.searchable_summary: sort and weigh pollen loads by color and appearance

steps.description: Microscopical examinations were conducted to identify the floral origin of pollen grains by comparing with the previously prepared reference pollen slide.
steps.searchable_summary: identify floral origin of pollen using microscopy

methods.method_name: Floral Resource Recording and Statistical Analysis
methods.description: Recording of plant species visited by honeybees and statistical analysis of collected data.

steps.description: Recording of all plant species which were observed to be visited by honeybee workers was done throughout the year in the ten regions represented Kaferelsheikh province.
steps.searchable_summary: record plant species visited by honeybees

steps.description: Unidentified plant species were collected, transferred to the laboratory, then were identified and recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and identify unknown plant species

steps.description: Data were statistically analyzed by the analysis of variance (ANOVA) using PROC GLM ver. 9.1.3 SAS software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA on collected data using SAS

steps.description: Means of monthly collected bee-pollen were compared using Least Significant Difference (LSD) tests (a: 0.05).
steps.searchable_summary: compare monthly pollen means using LSD test
